display.purpose: The hero greeting only. Appears exactly once on the site.

headline.purpose: Role titles in each experience section.

title.purpose: Company names (terracotta), project titles, sidebar wordmark.

body.purpose: Summaries and bullet copy at the 960px measure.

label.purpose: Periods, section eyebrows, sidebar group headers. Uppercase, tracked.

mono.purpose: Technical credit / data lines only — never as costume.

shadows.name: spotlight-lift
shadows.purpose: The single shadow in the system. Applied only to the floating spotlight panel and scaled by scroll progress to zero as it locks to full-bleed.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: The spotlight expand. Quick to open, settles gently into the lock. Disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Tag
components.description: Quiet skill/stack metadata under a role header.
components.css: .ds-tag { display: inline-block; background: var(--c-t10, #f2eded); color: var(--c-t50, #7d6464); font-family: var(--font-manrope), 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 9pt; font-weight: 500; border-radius: 3px; padding: 2px 8px; }

components.name: Sidebar Item
components.description: Left-rail work/contact link. Warm 70 at rest, terracotta on hover/focus.
components.css: .ds-sidebar-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 5px 0; text-decoration: none; font-family: var(--font-manrope), 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 9pt; color: var(--c-t70, #7a6060); border-radius: 2px; transition: color 0.15s ease; } .ds-sidebar-item:hover { color: var(--c-accent, #a05b4d); } .ds-sidebar-item:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--c-accent, #a05b4d); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-sidebar-year { margin-left: auto; font-size: 8.5pt; color: var(--c-t50, #7d6464); }

components.name: Spotlight Panel
components.description: Signature component. The headline role rests as an inset floating card and expands to full-bleed on scroll, its terracotta current-role rule fading in as it locks. Snippet shows the resting (floating) state.
components.html:
APR. 2025 – PRESENT

Design System Specialist

London Computer Systems
components.css: .ds-spotlight { position: relative; background: var(--c-t10, #f2eded); border-radius: 24px; box-shadow: 0 28px 72px -24px color-mix(in srgb, var(--c-type, #3B2F2F) 20%, transparent); padding: 64px 48px; overflow: hidden; font-family: var(--font-manrope), 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-spotlight-bar { position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; height: 3px; background: var(--c-accent, #a05b4d); } .ds-spotlight-eyebrow { margin: 0 0 8px; font-size: 10pt; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--c-t50, #7d6464); } .ds-spotlight-role { margin: 0 0 10px; font-size: 26pt; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: -0.02em; line-height: 1.1; color: var(--c-type, #3B2F2F); } .ds-spotlight-company { font-size: 14pt; font-weight: 500; color: var(--c-accent, #a05b4d); }

narrative.overview: A design-systems specialist's portfolio that behaves like a type-foundry specimen sheet crossed with a field notebook: everything catalogued, gridded, and consistent, but the materials are warm — earthen ink, terracotta, a single handwritten greeting. The rigor is the pitch; the warmth keeps it from reading as clinical. Restraint punctuated by a few earned signatures: a calm single-column editorial read with the drama concentrated into one moment — the current role expanding to a full-bleed spotlight — rather than scattered across every section.
keyCharacteristics: Warm ink on true white — never neutral gray, never cream-on-cream.
One handwritten voice, used exactly once.
Single-column editorial measure; hierarchy carries the argument.
One concentrated signature moment, not repeated flourishes.
Light and dark themes, both first-class.

rules.name: The Warm Ink Rule
rules.body: There are no neutral grays in this system. Every tint, divider, and muted text value is a brown-warmed step derived from the ink. A cool #888 gray anywhere is a bug.

rules.name: The Single Voice Rule
rules.body: Terracotta appears on a small fraction of any screen — company names, the live-role bar, active states, bullets. Its rarity is what makes it read as meaning rather than decoration.

rules.name: The One Script Rule
rules.body: Momo Signature is used exactly once — the hero greeting. The moment it appears twice it becomes decoration and the maker's-hand effect dies.

rules.name: The Grounded Spotlight Rule
rules.body: Shadow is never ambient or decorative. It exists on one element (the spotlight panel) and only as a function of scroll — full while floating, gone once the panel becomes the section.

dos: Do keep warmth in the ink and accent on a true-white page; all grays are brown-warmed washes of the ink.
Do reserve terracotta for meaning and keep it under ~10% of any screen — the Single Voice Rule.
Do use Momo Signature exactly once and let Manrope carry everything else through weight and size.
Do concentrate motion into one earned signature moment and let hierarchy make the seniority argument.
Do ship every effect with a prefers-reduced-motion fallback and verify both themes at WCAG AA.
donts: Don't drift toward the generic SaaS template: no hero-metric blocks, no identical feature-card grids, no gradient accents, no tracked-uppercase eyebrow above every section.
Don't repeat the spotlight expand (or any single entrance) on every section — the uniform-reflex / identical-card-grid tell is exactly what this system rejects.
Don't slide into Dribbble over-design: no glassmorphism as default, no decorative motion everywhere.
Don't let the supporting history collapse into a resume / CV dump with no point of view.
Don't reach for the trendy dark devtool look; mono is for technical credit lines only.
Don't introduce cool/neutral grays, a second display typeface, or ambient decorative shadows.
